commands.review_summary:
The branch adds model-id based Gemini detection in agent tool parameter schema normalization so Gemini schema cleanup also runs for OpenAI-compatible providers whose provider id is not Google/Gemini.

PR surface: Source +3. Total +3 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: no. high-confidence live reproduction was established in this read-only review. Source inspection shows current main can skip Gemini cleaning for provider jjcc with a Gemini model id, and the linked logs match raw cron anyOf fields that existing Gemini cleanup is meant to rewrite.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Maintainers should require contributor-supplied real behavior proof before merge; there is no narrow ClawSweeper repair because no discrete code defect was found.

target.title: fix(agents): apply Gemini schema cleaning for Gemini models via OpenAI-compat providers
target.body:
## Summary
**Problem:** `isGeminiProvider` only checked if the provider name contained "google" or "gemini". This missed OpenAI-compatible providers (like jjcc) that route to Gemini models, causing cron tool schema with `anyOf` to be rejected by Gemini's stricter validation.

**Fix:** Add model ID checking so Gemini schema cleaning is applied when the model ID contains "gemini", regardless of the provider name.

## Linked context
Closes #91542

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Cron tool schema with anyOf rejected by Gemini via OpenAI-compat providers
- Real environment tested: Windows 10, Node.js v22.11.0, OpenClaw latest main
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Verified the isGeminiProvider check now includes model ID
- Evidence after fix: Code change in `src/agents/agent-tools-parameter-schema.ts`:
```typescript
// Before:
const isGeminiProvider =
  normalizedProvider.includes("google") || normalizedProvider.includes("gemini");

// After:
const isGeminiProvider =
  normalizedProvider.includes("google") ||
  normalizedProvider.includes("gemini") ||
  normalizedModel.includes("gemini");
```
- Observed result after fix: Gemini schema cleaning is applied for Gemini models via any provider
- What was not tested: Live jjcc/Gemini request (requires jjcc credentials)

## Tests and validation
- No code changes beyond the provider detection logic
- No CHANGELOG.md edits

## Risk checklist
- userVisible: No (internal schema processing)
- configEnvMigration: No
- securityAuthNetwork: No
- Mitigation: Only extends existing Gemini detection; non-Gemini models unaffected

## Current review state
- [x] AI-assisted (built with Claude Code)
